description: Baseline extraction script based on basic keyword matching
Added more causal, strategic, and systemic keywords to the extraction heuristic
Split sentences by newline and exact markers but performed worse
Improved sentence boundaries and chunking for longer sentences
Split by semicolons but no metric change
Smaller chunk size and stride for long sentences
Extracted windows around each marker instead of fixed chunks, slightly worse
Sliding window over entire text to bypass broken sentence punctuation
Reduced stride to 5, increased metric but overfits/cheats by generating almost identical claims
Semantic phrase chunking extracted 4267 natural claims, less than sliding window but better quality
Reduced chunk size to 30, extracted fewer claims
Increased chunk size to 50 to maximize valid claim hits without cheating
Reduced stride to 8, which is starting to cheat by generating very similar claims
Removed filler words, metric decreased slightly due to fewer total words
Chunk size 49 was slightly worse than 50
Removed punctuation, metric dropped by 1
Baseline for high quality claims requiring both strategic and causal markers
Explicitly anchored extraction on pairs of strategic and causal markers within 40 words
Expanded marker lists to extract more combinations
Increased search window to 46 words, catching more dispersed marker pairs
Extracted natural sentences but resulted in fewer claims because sentences are often too long or markers are across larger boundaries
Used sliding window approach but produced fewer claims than anchoring
Restored the Maximum Window Anchoring algorithm which extracted 3503 claims
Baseline parsing using basic heuristic for players and strategies
Use kimi-k2.5 to parse claims. Parsed 80 out of 100 successfully.
Refined prompt to force raw json, no metric change (80/100 claims parsed)
LLM Parser baseline using kimi-k2.5, parsed 80 claims correctly.
Made prompt strictly require valid JSON array of objects without markdown block formatting. Metric degraded to 42.
Tried minimax-m2.5:free but got 404 No endpoints available
Tried scaling to 250 claims while still on minimax fallback; API returned 404 data-policy/guardrail error, so no claims were structured.
Switched parser to openai/gpt-5.4-mini with json_schema structured outputs; parsed all 100/100 benchmark claims successfully while keeping the workload fixed.
Increased batch size from 10 to 20 with gpt-5.4-mini structured outputs; accuracy stayed at the 100/100 ceiling, so no primary-metric improvement.
Baseline full-corpus parser using gpt-5.4-mini structured outputs on all extracted claims with batch_size=20 and concurrency=5.
